mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive mean 60-day forward return, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases yields positive median 20-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power when regime is misclassified.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative mean 20-day forward return, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading above 105.0 indicates robust manufacturing and utility output that alleviates supply chain bottlenecks, reducing cost pressures and triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of months where INDPRO exceeds 105.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
